name: dbos-typescript
description: DBOS TypeScript SDK for building reliable, fault-tolerant applications with durable workflows. Use this skill when writing TypeScript code with DBOS, creating workflows and steps, using queues, using DBOSClient from external applications, or building applications that need to be resilient to failures.
capabilities: Guide DBOS workflow and step creation with proper patterns
Configure DBOS applications with lifecycle management
Implement queue-based concurrency with rate limiting
Set up workflow communication via events, messages, and streams
Apply idempotency and determinism patterns for reliable recovery
Test DBOS applications with proper mocking strategies
author: sickn33
securitySummary: This is a legitimate documentation skill for DBOS TypeScript SDK. Static findings are false positives triggered by markdown syntax (code blocks using backticks), standard configuration patterns (environment variables), and documentation keywords. No actual security vulnerabilities present.
valueStatement: DBOS TypeScript SDK enables developers to build reliable applications with durable workflows that automatically recover from failures. The skill provides best practices for workflows, steps, queues, and communication patterns.
limitations: Does not include actual DBOS SDK runtime code
Does not provide database connection management
Does not cover deployment or infrastructure setup
Focuses on patterns rather than complete application examples

useCases.title: Build resilient data processing pipelines
useCases.description: Create workflows that reliably process data with automatic retry and recovery from failures

useCases.title: Implement reliable microservice communication
useCases.description: Use DBOS events and messages for decoupled, fault-tolerant inter-service communication

useCases.title: Add durability to existing Node.js applications
useCases.description: Enhance TypeScript applications with durable workflow execution that survives crashes and restarts

promptTemplates.title: Create a basic DBOS workflow
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a DBOS workflow that fetches data from an API and processes it. Show how to use DBOS.runStep for the API call and register the workflow properly.

promptTemplates.title: Implement workflow with retry logic
promptTemplates.prompt: Show how to configure step retries with exponential backoff for a DBOS workflow that calls an unreliable external service.

promptTemplates.title: Set up workflow queues
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a DBOS queue with concurrency limits and rate limiting for processing items. Show how to enqueue workflows and configure worker behavior.

promptTemplates.title: Implement workflow communication
promptTemplates.prompt: Demonstrate how to use DBOS events for inter-workflow communication. Show how one workflow can emit events and another can listen for them.

bestPractices: Keep workflows deterministic - move all non-deterministic operations to steps
Always use DBOS.runStep for any operation that accesses external services or databases
Configure appropriate retry policies for steps that may fail transiently
antiPatterns: Calling workflows from within steps - this breaks the execution model
Using Math.random() or Date.now() directly in workflows - they cause non-determinism
Modifying global variables from workflows - this breaks replay capability

faq.question: What is DBOS?
faq.answer: DBOS (Database-Oriented Operating System) is a framework that provides durable workflow execution for TypeScript applications, ensuring reliability and automatic recovery from failures.

faq.question: How does DBOS ensure reliability?
faq.answer: DBOS checkpointing records workflow state, allowing automatic replay and recovery after crashes or restarts without data loss.

faq.question: When should I use a step vs a workflow?
faq.answer: Use workflows for orchestration and decision-making. Use steps for operations that are non-deterministic, like API calls, file operations, or database queries.

faq.question: Can I call external APIs from workflows?
faq.answer: No, external API calls must be wrapped in DBOS.runStep. Direct API calls in workflows break determinism and recovery.

faq.question: How do I handle failures in DBOS?
faq.answer: Configure retry policies on steps using the retries option in runStep. Workflows automatically retry from the last checkpoint on failure.

faq.question: Does DBOS work with any database?
faq.answer: DBOS supports PostgreSQL, MySQL, and SQLite databases through its transaction system.
